title: Evangelion is a Video Game Character
display_title: Evangelion is a Video Game Character
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion is a Video Game Character             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Feb 13, 2008 8:09 pm http://www.japantoday.com/jp/shukan/230 The article wrote: Local landmark Radio Hall, located in front of the Denki-gai exit of JR Akihabara station, always carries the most popular products of each generation. Now, they have plastic model figures of beautiful girls from TV animated series and superhero robots for hardcore fans. There is a 380,000 yen life-size Evangelion (video game character) figure , as well as perennial favorites Gozilla, Kamen Rider, Ultra Man and Kikaida.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 03, 2008 5:39 pm Amusing, yes. Though I don't understand what's the appeal of hella-expensive life size figures. *Notices the tumbleweeds blowing by of the otherwise empty thread*

[]: Re: Evangelion is a Video Game Character             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Mar 03, 2008 5:44 pm NAveryW wrote: There is a 380,000 yen life-size Evangelion (video game character) figure

[]: Re: Evangelion is a Video Game Character             Quote Post by honsou » Tue Mar 04, 2008 1:25 am CorporalChaos wrote: NAveryW wrote: There is a 380,000 yen life-size Evangelion (video game character) figure

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Mar 04, 2008 11:47 am SPARTAN117CJL posted something funny that's similar on ANF: a Chinese model of Eva-01 -

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Tue Mar 04, 2008 12:06 pm What they flying-fuck! Seriously tho', renaming the angels 'envoys' completely bastardises the series.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Tue Mar 04, 2008 3:28 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: Seriously tho', renaming the angels 'envoys' completely bastardises the series.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Mar 04, 2008 6:17 pm Zugzwang wrote: <snippy>

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Tue Mar 04, 2008 8:29 pm What the... Sky Hawk Fighter? Impetuous and crude flying bird? Were these people using a corrupted version of Babelfish? Only 6 bucks though...

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Mar 04, 2008 8:32 pm I know its levels of Engrish are OVER 9000!!!!!!! (sorry)

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Wed Mar 05, 2008 2:32 am BobBQ wrote: Indigo Arcangel wrote: Seriously tho', renaming the angels 'envoys' completely bastardises the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Mar 05, 2008 10:22 am RandomStuff wrote: Impetuous and crude flying bird?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 05, 2008 10:51 am Reminds me of the camprip of the first Death Note movie that I saw, which was Japanese translated to Chinese, which then someone translated to English (not very well). Towards the end, so much of the subtitles were completel gibberish. Like calling someone a turkey, or a fire truck, or something that made no sense. It's possible that it's just a mistranslation, since a large amount of nouns in Mandarin are compound words (turkey = fire + chicken, automobile = steam/air + vehicle, subway = metal + [below]ground), so a too literal translation of a noun can end up sounding like gibberish.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Mar 05, 2008 2:46 pm "You only think of me as food." Well, someone had to say it.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Mar 05, 2008 4:45 pm IT'S CHIEF DING! Ohhh baby...
